mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising housing starts indicates broad-based economic strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in over 60% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to risk-free assets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show a positive correlation with RSAFS increases, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index increase exceeding 2.5% year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% annual threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains and weak demand, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO drops below the 0.5% threshold during recovery regimes.
